response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced full-stack engineer with an impressive breadth across web, mobile, and embedded work, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. His C++ experience amounts to roughly a year (National Instruments plus a thesis project) against a hard requirement of 8 years, his large-scale distributed systems exposure is limited to about two years at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (primarily in DLang), and he has no formal technical leadership experience mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers on a compute platform. His strengths lie in product-oriented full-stack development and teaching, not in leading technical strategy for internal cloud compute infrastructure.
strengths: Genuine high-throughput systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (low-latency services handling millions of requests per second)
15+ years of broad engineering experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with strong testing and craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD)
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, courses, and conference talks, plus founder-level ownership of GISCollective
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ requirement — only ~7 months of professional C/C++ work in 2014-2015
No production cloud compute platform or cloud architecture leadership experience; recent work is product-focused full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js)
No formal technical leadership role leading strategy, priorities, and cross-team alignment; leadership evidence is informal (workshops, open source)

key_factors.factor: Limited large-scale infrastructure and cloud architecture depth
High-throughput distributed systems and mentoring background
